MustExist Attribute
The [MustExist] attribute marks a perspective Apply method as requiring the model to already exist When applied, the generated runner code includes a null check before calling the

OrderView Apply(OrderView current, OrderShipped @event) { return current with { Status = "Shipped", ShippedAt = @event ShippedAt }; } } ` Parameter Nullability Use the nullable annotation to signal in

Cascade Context
CascadeContext provides unified context propagation for message flows in Whizbang It encapsulates the essential data that should cascade from parent to child messages: CorrelationId, C

message SB->>HOP: Extract SecurityContext HOP->>MC_B: Create IMessageContext
(SOURCE restored) MC_B->>SB: Set as InitiatingContext Note over SB: SB has full context
as if request started here

Metrics Configuration
MetricsOptions provides runtime configuration for the Whizbang metrics system Configure which components emit metrics, control tag cardinality, and customize histogram buckets Qu

Default buckets optimized for typical handler durations options Metrics DurationBuckets = [1, 5, 10, 25, 50, 100, 250, 500, 1000, 2500, 5000, 10000]; // Custom buckets for fast operations options Metr

Handler Metrics
HandlerMetrics provides instrumentation for tracking handler invocations, execution duration, and outcomes It integrates with OpenTelemetry-compatible observability backends IHandlerMe

| Total handler invocations | | whizbang handler successes | Counter | Successful completions | | whizbang handler failures | Counter | Failed executions | | whizbang handler early_returns | Counter |

Aspire Handler metrics appear in the Aspire dashboard automatically: `csharp var builder = DistributedApplication CreateBuilder(args); builder AddOpenTelemetry() WithMetricsExporter(); ` Grafana Dashb

Metrics Overview
WhizbangMetrics provides a comprehensive set of OpenTelemetry-compatible metrics instruments for monitoring your event-driven applications All metrics are exposed through the standard
See Metric Components for details --- Metric Instruments Whizbang provides 44 metric instruments across all components: Handler Metrics | Metric | Type | Description | |--------|------|-------------|
Histogram | Batch sizes | | whizbang outbox delivery_latency | Histogram | Time to delivery | Inbox Metrics | Metric | Type | Description | |--------|------|-------------| | whizbang inbox received |

Trace Components
TraceComponents is a flags enum that controls which parts of the system generate trace output Use it to focus on specific areas during debugging without being overwhelmed by unrelated

start Handler completion (duration, result) Use when: Debugging handler selection or execution Lifecycle (128) Traces lifecycle stages: PreExecute phase Execute phase PostExecute phase Stage transitio

Tracing Overview
Whizbang provides comprehensive distributed tracing across the entire message lifecycle Traces flow from HTTP requests through handlers to perspectives, giving complete visibility int

message | | Config: TracedHandlers | Runtime | Target specific handlers by name | | Config: TracedMessages | Runtime | Target specific messages by name | | Config: Verbosity | Runtime | Global baselin

trace source | attribute or config | | whizbang handler | Handler name | | whizbang message_type | Message type name | | whizbang duration_ms | Duration in milliseconds | | whizbang status | Completio

last non-null TraceParent from message hops Spans are parented correctly: Child spans are linked to the original request trace, even across process boundaries Code Implementation `csharp // Extract pa

| whizbang perspective event_types | Event types with counts (e g , "OrderCreated:3, OrderUpdated:2") | This gives you visibility into event processing without the overhead of per-event spans --- Pers

Detail Use in: Local development only `json { "Whizbang": { "Tracing": { "Verbosity": "Debug" } } } ` What's traced (includes Verbose): Full message payloads (serialized JSON) Timing breakdown per ope
